_comment: Windows Maintenance Automation - main settings. Edit the values below to change behavior; no code changes needed. This file is read once per run by the orchestrator (Get-MainConfig) and never prompts for input, so every setting here is safe to use for fully unattended/Task Scheduler execution.

shutdown._comment: Controls Stage 5 (the only stage that can reboot or delete the project folder).
shutdown._rebootOnlyWhenRequired_note: When true (recommended), a reboot only happens if a Type2 module actually flagged one as required (e.g. Windows Update, a Defender policy change). When false, rebootOnTimeout alone decides.

modules._comment: Set any flag below to true to SKIP that module's Type2 action (install/remove/change) for every run, without editing any code. The corresponding Type1 audit still runs and still reports findings in the HTML report - only the automatic fix is skipped. This is read by MaintenanceOrchestrator.ps1 in Stage 2 and requires no user interaction, so it is safe to use with -NonInteractive / Task Scheduler.
modules._skipBloatwareRemoval_desc: Skip removing bloatware/OEM AppX packages found by the bloatware audit.
modules._skipAppManagement_desc: Skip app management (install missing essential apps and upgrade outdated apps). Audits both install and upgrade scenarios in a single pass.
modules._skipWindowsUpdates_desc: Skip installing pending Windows Updates found by the update audit.
modules._skipSystemHardening_desc: Skip system hardening (security baseline + telemetry/privacy disabling). Includes: Defender settings, firewall, registry hardening, disabling telemetry services and tasks.
modules._skipDiskCleanup_desc: Skip clearing temp files, browser cache/cookies, Windows Update component cleanup, and Recycle Bin. See config/lists/disk-cleanup/disk-cleanup-config.json for finer-grained toggles (e.g. cookie clearing is off by default there).
